title: Architrave per-repo config
description: Declares how Architrave retargets its agents, gates, and knowledge for one repo. Lives at the repo root as architrave.config.json. UI, backend, full-stack, and infrastructure lanes are configured here; infra is always plan-only.

platform.description: Selects the knowledge pack the Platform Design agent loads.

stack.description: UI framework used to implement the design natively.

designSource.description: Where the canonical design lives (the thing to reproduce, never reinvent).

path.description: Repo-relative path to the design workbench (e.g. src/Admin/.storybook, ui-lab).

url.description: Optional running/published Storybook URL (e.g. http://localhost:6007 or a gh-pages demo).

spec.description: Optional repo-relative path to a written design spec (e.g. docs/ui/*-design-spec.md).

mcp.description: Optional Storybook MCP endpoint from @storybook/addon-mcp (e.g. http://localhost:6006/mcp). Intended for React/web Storybook 10.3+ workbenches; omit for native-only previews unless a compatible MCP endpoint exists. When set, agents ground via list-all-documentation/get-documentation, write stories via get-storybook-story-instructions, and preview for sign-off via preview-stories.

designMap.description: Optional repo-relative path to the component glossary / ui-map (e.g. docs/design/phonodeck-ui-map.json). Strongly recommended for UI lanes; maps product/component names to Storybook stories, code paths, states, tokens, and capability claims.

tokens.description: Optional repo-relative path to design token file(s), commonly *.tokens.json. When set with tokenBuild, reconcile can mechanically validate design<->code drift; when omitted, Storybook/specs remain the design source and reconcile skips token regeneration.

tokenBuild.description: Optional command that regenerates platform code from tokens (e.g. Style Dictionary / Terrazzo). Used by the reconcile gate.

knowledgePack.description: Override which knowledge pack to load. Defaults to the one implied by platform.

applyTo.description: Globs where the UI lane lives. Scopes UI instructions/agents inside a mixed repo so backend/infra lanes can stay separately governed.

build.description: Build command (e.g. 'make build', 'npm run build', 'dotnet build').

test.description: Test command (e.g. 'make test', 'npx playwright test', 'dotnet test').

screenshot.description: Optional command/recipe that captures a screenshot to compare against the Storybook reference.

backend.description: Optional service/backend tier. When set, the Architrave harness extends to the backend lane (Service Architect / Backend Planner / Backend Implementer) gated by the same Adversarial Judge. Omit for repos without a service tier.

solution.description: Repo-relative path to the solution/workspace root the implementer grounds in (e.g. 'src', 'Tessera.slnx').

architectureDocs.description: Repo-relative paths to the architecture truth the backend agents ground in (ADRs, architecture.md) - the backend's 'designSource'. Reproduce these patterns; never invent parallel abstractions.

contracts.description: Optional repo-relative path to the API/data contracts (OpenAPI, proto, DTOs) - the cross-tier handshake UI and backend both honor.

applyTo.description: Globs where the backend lives (scopes the backend lane).

iac.description: Optional infrastructure-as-code lane. The Infra Engineer is PLAN-ONLY by charter: it proposes diffs + plan/policy output and NEVER applies - apply stays a human action. Highest-blast-radius lane (secrets, identity, network).

plan.description: Plan/preview command - NEVER apply (e.g. 'kubectl diff -k deploy/k8s', 'az deployment group what-if', 'terraform plan').

ops.description: Optional runtime/operations observation lane. Used by Runtime Observer for read-only deployment/runtime evidence (for example Homelab MCP, Kubernetes health, logs, ingress, Flux state). Runtime mutations require explicit human approval.

mode.description: Default access mode. Use read-only unless the repo intentionally supports approval-gated operations.

mcpServer.description: Optional MCP server name, e.g. homelab. Agents use it only if available in the client.

purpose.description: Allowed observation purposes such as runtime-health, logs, deployment-verification, version-drift.

requiresApprovalFor.description: Operations that require explicit human approval, e.g. mutations, reconcile, restart, secret-access, network-change.

learning.description: Optional learning-loop settings. Durable run artifacts, repo profile, and repeated repo lessons live outside the config; stable lessons are promoted into config/docs only after review.

runArtifactsPath.description: Repo-relative directory for per-run audit artifacts, e.g. .architrave/runs.

repoProfilePath.description: Repo-relative markdown file for the concise validated repo description, e.g. .architrave/learning/repo-profile.md.

lessonsPath.description: Repo-relative markdown file for durable repeated lessons, e.g. .architrave/learning/repo-lessons.md.

capture.description: Artifact categories Architrave should keep for non-trivial runs.

redactionPolicy.description: How learning artifacts handle sensitive data. Use no-secrets unless the repo has a stricter custom policy.

staleFactPolicy.description: How persisted facts are trusted before use or promotion.

promotionPolicy.description: How repeated lessons become durable repo instructions/config.

promoteAfterOccurrences.description: How many repeated observations before Architrave should propose promotion into config/AGENTS/docs.

promoteTargets.description: Allowed durable destinations for promoted lessons.
